reasoning: The starting BBB OAS value is essentially constrained at a historical floor of 1.01%. Downward movement is highly limited because spreads rarely break below 1.00%, creating an asymmetric upward risk profile. In the short term, early January is canonically the busiest month for new corporate bond issuance; this sudden jump in supply routinely creates a "new issue premium" that drives moderate, temporary spread widening (analogous to the rise to 1.03% in early January 2025). The FRED seasonal analysis model correspondingly assigns a 67% probability of exceeding the threshold by January 11. Consequently, I establish a short-term probability of 0.64 for the first resolution date. For the more distant horizons (February and April), I strictly adhere to the prompt's mandated instruction that uncertainty must increase with horizon length, drawing the subsequent baseline probabilities progressively closer to 50/50 (0.57 for February, 0.53 for April).
